extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true whenever asthma is true (rule rec_b_from_trigger, pri 50). rec_drug_a is set true only when the 10-term conjunction holds (rule rec_a_from_conjunction, pri 50), and that conjunction INCLUDES asthma as one of its conjuncts. Therefore any input that makes rec_drug_a true necessarily also makes asthma true, which in turn makes rec_drug_b true. The two rules write to different output variables (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b) with no competing rules, so priorities/ties never suppress either — both fire independently. Setting all ten conjuncts true (asthma, peptic_ulcer, elderly, qt_prolongation, bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, anticoagulated, renal_impairment, heart_failure, immunosuppressed) makes both rec_drug_a := true and rec_drug_b := true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. ckd_dialysis only affects rec_drug_c and is irrelevant (set false).
